description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, slightly beveled wooden case and a classic mid-20th-century design. The front panel features a large, honeycomb-patterned speaker grille in a light tan color, occupying the upper half, with a single round knob on the left. Below the grille, there is a control panel with two black and gold rotary knobs on either side and a horizontal frequency display in the center, marked with various radio bands. The sides and top are finished in a dark, polished wood grain, giving it a sturdy and elegant appearance. The back and bottom are plain, with no visible controls or openings. This radio receiver would typically be found in homes, shops, or public spaces as a source of music and news.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a honeycomb speaker grille, two rotary knobs, and a central frequency display, all set in a dark, polished case.
description_view_0: The front view shows the honeycomb-patterned speaker grille, two rotary knobs, and the frequency display panel.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the smooth, dark wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, flat panel with no visible openings or details.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the same dark wooden finish without controls.
